title: My School Project
summary: A teenage boy describes the science project he is working on for school, explaining what he has researched, what he has built, and how he plans to present it.
body: This term at school, we have to complete a big science project and present it to the class. My project is about renewable energy, and I have been working on it for the past three weeks. I decided to focus on solar power because I think it is one of the most exciting forms of clean energy. To start with, I spent a few days doing research online and in the school library. I made notes about how solar panels work and why they are important for the environment. After the research stage, I built a simple model of a solar-powered house using cardboard, wire, and a small solar panel I ordered online. When the sun shines on the panel, a tiny LED light inside the model house turns on, which is really satisfying to watch. My project also includes a poster with charts and diagrams that explain how solar energy is collected and converted into electricity. I have practised my presentation several times in front of my mirror at home. I am still a little nervous about speaking in front of the class, but my teacher says the most important thing is to speak clearly and not to rush. I hope my classmates find the topic as interesting as I do. The project has taught me that renewable energy could help solve many of the world's environmental problems, and I want to learn more about it in the future.

annotations.exampleEn: Solar and wind power are popular forms of renewable energy.
Many homes in sunny countries use solar power.
The report focuses on air pollution in cities.
I made notes during the lecture to help me study later.

annotations.meaningVi: tấm pin năng lượng mặt trời
annotations.exampleEn: Solar panels on the roof generate electricity for the whole house.
He built a model of the bridge using cardboard and glue.
Solar energy is converted into electricity by the panels.
Remember to speak clearly so everyone at the back can hear you.
Take your time and try not to rush through the presentation.
Climate change is one of the biggest environmental problems today.
I hope to travel to many countries in the future.

grammarNotes.title: Present perfect for experience and actions connected to the present: 'I have been working on it for three weeks'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng hiện tại hoàn thành tiếp diễn (have/has been + verb-ing) để nói về hành động bắt đầu trong quá khứ và vẫn đang tiếp tục đến hiện tại. 'I have been working on it for the past three weeks' — Tôi đã làm dự án này được ba tuần và vẫn đang tiếp tục. Dùng 'for' để nói về khoảng thời gian. So sánh: 'I have practised my presentation several times' (hiện tại hoàn thành đơn — hành động đã xảy ra nhiều lần, kết quả còn liên quan đến hiện tại).
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use the present perfect continuous (have/has been + verb-ing) for an action that started in the past and is still continuing. 'I have been working on it for the past three weeks' — it started three weeks ago and is still in progress. Use 'for' with a time period. Compare the present perfect simple: 'I have practised my presentation several times' — a completed action, but its result (being prepared) is relevant now. Both tenses connect past actions to the present moment.

grammarNotes.title: 'I decided to' and 'I want to' + infinitive: expressing decisions and wishes
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Sau 'decide' và 'want', dùng 'to + động từ nguyên thể'. 'I decided to focus on solar power.' 'I want to learn more about it.' Đây là hai động từ rất thông dụng: 'decide to' (quyết định làm gì) và 'want to' (muốn làm gì). Các động từ tương tự cũng theo cấu trúc 'verb + to + infinitive': plan to, hope to, need to, agree to, try to. 'I hope to learn more' / 'I plan to practise more' — đây là những cấu trúc thiết yếu ở cấp độ A2.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: After 'decide' and 'want', use 'to + bare infinitive'. Examples: 'I decided to focus on solar power.' / 'I want to learn more.' Other common verbs that follow this pattern: plan to, hope to, need to, agree to, try to, manage to. Examples: 'I hope to do well.' / 'I need to practise more.' This pattern — verb + to + infinitive — is one of the most important structures at A2 level.

translationVi: Học kỳ này ở trường, chúng tôi phải hoàn thành một dự án khoa học lớn và trình bày trước lớp. Dự án của tôi về năng lượng tái tạo, và tôi đã làm dự án này trong ba tuần qua. Tôi quyết định tập trung vào năng lượng mặt trời vì tôi nghĩ đây là một trong những dạng năng lượng sạch thú vị nhất. Để bắt đầu, tôi dành vài ngày nghiên cứu trực tuyến và trong thư viện trường. Tôi ghi chép về cách tấm pin năng lượng mặt trời hoạt động và tại sao chúng quan trọng với môi trường. Sau giai đoạn nghiên cứu, tôi xây dựng một mô hình đơn giản của một ngôi nhà dùng năng lượng mặt trời bằng bìa cứng, dây điện và một tấm pin mặt trời nhỏ tôi đặt mua trực tuyến. Khi mặt trời chiếu vào tấm pin, một đèn LED nhỏ bên trong mô hình nhà bật sáng, rất thỏa mãn khi nhìn vào. Dự án của tôi cũng có một tấm áp phích với biểu đồ và sơ đồ giải thích cách thu thập và chuyển đổi năng lượng mặt trời thành điện. Tôi đã tập trình bày nhiều lần trước gương ở nhà. Tôi vẫn hơi lo lắng về việc nói trước lớp, nhưng giáo viên của tôi nói điều quan trọng nhất là nói rõ ràng và không vội vàng. Tôi hy vọng các bạn cùng lớp thấy chủ đề này thú vị như tôi. Dự án đã dạy tôi rằng năng lượng tái tạo có thể giúp giải quyết nhiều vấn đề môi trường của thế giới, và tôi muốn tìm hiểu thêm về nó trong tương lai.

questions.prompt: What does the narrator have to do at school this term?
options: Complete a science project and present it
questions.explanation: 'We have to complete a big science project and present it to the class.'
questions.hint: He mentions both completing and presenting.

questions.prompt: What is the narrator's project topic?
options: Renewable energy with a focus on solar power
questions.explanation: 'My project is about renewable energy, and I decided to focus on solar power.'
questions.hint: He gives both a broad topic and a specific focus.

questions.prompt: How long has the narrator been working on his project?
questions.explanation: 'I have been working on it for the past three weeks.'

questions.prompt: Where did the narrator do his research?
options: Online and in the school library
questions.explanation: 'I spent a few days doing research online and in the school library.'

questions.prompt: What did the narrator make notes about?
options: How solar panels work and why they are important for the environment
Famous scientists who work on energy
questions.explanation: 'I made notes about how solar panels work and why they are important for the environment.'
questions.hint: Two things are mentioned in his notes.

questions.prompt: What did the narrator build for his project?
options: A simple model of a solar-powered house
questions.explanation: 'I built a simple model of a solar-powered house.'
questions.hint: It is a model of a building.

questions.prompt: What materials did the narrator use to build the model?
options: Cardboard, wire, and a small solar panel
questions.explanation: 'Using cardboard, wire, and a small solar panel I ordered online.'

questions.prompt: What happens when the sun shines on the model's solar panel?
options: A tiny LED light turns on
questions.explanation: 'When the sun shines on the panel, a tiny LED light inside the model house turns on.'
questions.hint: It is a small light source.

questions.prompt: What else does the project include besides the model?
options: A poster with charts and diagrams
questions.explanation: 'My project also includes a poster with charts and diagrams.'

questions.prompt: How has the narrator practised his presentation?
options: In front of his mirror at home
By recording himself on his phone
questions.explanation: 'I have practised my presentation several times in front of my mirror at home.'

questions.prompt: What advice does the narrator's teacher give about presentations?
questions.explanation: 'My teacher says the most important thing is to speak clearly and not to rush.'
questions.hint: Two pieces of advice are given.

questions.prompt: What has the project taught the narrator?
options: That solar power is too expensive
That renewable energy could help solve environmental problems
That he prefers history to science
questions.explanation: 'The project has taught me that renewable energy could help solve many of the world's environmental problems.'
questions.hint: He mentions a positive lesson about energy.

cloze.template: This term at school, we have to complete a big science project and {{b1}} it to the class. My project is about renewable energy, and I have been working on it for the past three weeks. I decided to {{b2}} on solar power because I think it is one of the most exciting forms of clean energy. To start with, I spent a few days doing {{b3}} online and in the school library. I made notes about how solar {{b4}} work and why they are important for the environment. After the research stage, I built a simple model of a solar-powered house using {{b5}}, wire, and a small solar panel I ordered online. When the sun shines on the panel, a tiny LED {{b6}} inside the model house turns on, which is really satisfying to watch. My project also includes a {{b7}} with charts and diagrams that explain how solar energy is collected and converted into electricity. I have {{b8}} my presentation several times in front of my mirror at home. I am still a little {{b9}} about speaking in front of the class, but my teacher says the most important thing is to speak clearly and not to rush. The project has taught me that renewable energy could help solve many of the world's {{b10}} problems.

blanks.explanation: 'present' — students present their work to the class.
'focus' — 'focus on' means to give your main attention to something.
'research' — 'doing research' means finding information about a topic.
'panels' — 'solar panels' are the devices that collect sunlight.
'cardboard' — the model was built using 'cardboard, wire, and a small solar panel.'
'light' — 'a tiny LED light' turns on inside the model house.
'poster' — the project includes a 'poster with charts and diagrams.'
'practised' — 'I have practised my presentation several times.'
'nervous' — the passage says 'I am still a little nervous about speaking in front of the class.'
'environmental' — the passage says 'environmental problems' relating to nature and the planet.

criticalThinkingQuestion: The narrator is excited about renewable energy and wants to learn more about it in the future. Do you think schools should teach students more about environmental issues and clean energy? What can young people do to help the environment in their daily lives?
